title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Big screen or small screen? Write an article comparing the experience of going to the cinema with watching films at home through a streaming service. Which do you prefer, and why?'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Popcorn, Pixels, and the Perfect Film Experience

In an era when virtually every film is available at home within weeks of release, the question of whether the cinema still matters has never been more relevant — or more divisive.

The case for streaming is undeniable. Watching at home is cheaper, more comfortable, and far more convenient. You can pause, rewind, wear pyjamas, and choose from thousands of films at any moment. For those with busy lives, this flexibility is genuinely invaluable.

And yet, something irreplaceable is lost when a film shrinks from a six-metre screen to a laptop. Cinema offers a kind of total immersion — the vast image, the enveloping sound, the shared emotional experience with a room full of strangers — that simply cannot be replicated on a sofa. Some films demand to be seen on a big screen to be fully understood.

Personally, I believe both experiences have their place. For a Friday-evening thriller, streaming is perfect. But for a genuine cinematic event, nothing rivals the ritual of the cinema. The dark room, the anticipation, the collective gasps and laughter — that is something worth preserving.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You have heard that the independent cinema in your town is at risk of closing down due to lack of funding. Write a letter to your local council arguing that the cinema should be supported financially, explaining what it contributes to the community and suggesting practical ways the council could help.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Members of the Council,

I am writing to urge you to provide financial support to the Rialto Cinema, which I understand faces closure without immediate assistance. I believe losing this venue would represent a serious and lasting loss for our town.

The Rialto is far more than a place to watch films. It is a genuine cultural hub that has served our community for over forty years. It screens independent and foreign-language films that would otherwise be unavailable locally, hosts school trips and educational screenings, and provides a unique social space for residents of all ages. The closure of such a venue would impoverish the cultural life of our town considerably.

I would respectfully suggest several ways the council could help. A direct annual subsidy, comparable to the support already provided to the town theatre, would stabilise the cinema's finances immediately. Alternatively, a reduced business rates arrangement or a one-off capital grant for essential building repairs would provide vital breathing space.

I sincerely hope the council will act swiftly to ensure this irreplaceable venue survives for future generations.

Yours sincerely,
A Local Resident

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Jamie has not seen any good films recently and has asked you for recommendations. Write an email to Jamie recommending a specific film, describing what it is about, explaining why you enjoyed it, and saying why you think Jamie in particular would enjoy it.

Write your email to Jamie.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Jamie,

I am so glad you asked — I actually watched the most brilliant film last week and have not stopped thinking about it since. It is called Parasite, directed by Bong Joon-ho, and it won the Academy Award for Best Picture a few years ago. I know it sounds unlikely, but please bear with me!

It is a South Korean thriller about a poor family who gradually infiltrate the lives of an extremely wealthy household. What sounds like a straightforward social satire quickly becomes something far more gripping, surprising, and emotionally complex. Without giving anything away, there is a twist roughly halfway through that genuinely left me speechless.

I think you would love it particularly because you are always saying you want to watch something unpredictable. It is funny, tense, heartbreaking, and thought-provoking — sometimes all within the same scene. The performances are extraordinary, and the direction is absolutely masterful.

Yes, it has subtitles, but honestly you forget about them after the first five minutes.

Trust me on this one — watch it this weekend and let me know what you think!

Best,
Riley
